title: auricula glutinosa
intro: Auricula glutinosa, also known as sticky mouse-ear or sticky alpine balsam, is a flowering plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its sticky, glandular leaves and stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of Auricula glutinosa include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system, making it a popular natural remedy for colds and coughs. Auricula glutinosa can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to treat various health issues, and is also used in the production of herbal medicines, such as cough syrups and respiratory tonics.
